claimBoundary: This inventory is a generated Skill Control Plane read model. It classifies skill surfaces for governance review only. It is not canonical certification authority, package body authority, provider runtime authority, Web runtime authority, public-sync evidence, or permission to activate, execute, commit, push, or bypass governed work-order scope.

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Source-backed spec before non-trivial implementation.
notRecommendedWhen: the task is a direct tiny fix with clear acceptance criteria
recommendedWhen: the spec is ambiguous, high-risk, or needs a written implementation contract before code

registry.name: CVF Engineering Planning And Task Breakdown

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Ordered, dependency-aware, testable task breakdown.
notRecommendedWhen: implementation steps are already obvious and tiny
recommendedWhen: the spec asks for a roadmap, plan, task breakdown, or sequencing before execution

registry.name: CVF Engineering API And Interface Design

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Clear API or interface contracts before implementation.
notRecommendedWhen: task is mainly visual UI implementation
task is mainly runtime debugging
recommendedWhen: the spec names API endpoints, module contracts, schemas, or component interfaces

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Browser runtime evidence for UI or web behavior.
notRecommendedWhen: no browser surface exists
the request is only static documentation
recommendedWhen: the spec requires verifying a web page, DOM behavior, console errors, or screenshots

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Repeatable quality gates and pipeline evidence.
notRecommendedWhen: task is a local-only code edit without automation impact
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions CI, Github Actions, pipeline checks, deployment gates, or automated verification

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Finding-first code review across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance.
notRecommendedWhen: the user asks for implementation without review
recommendedWhen: the spec asks to review a diff, inspect another agent's work, or judge implementation quality

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Behavior-preserving simplification with tests and local conventions.
notRecommendedWhen: behavior is not understood
the user asks for new feature expansion
recommendedWhen: the spec asks to refactor, simplify, remove duplication, or improve maintainability

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Correct startup and context handling without treating provider memory as CVF authority.
notRecommendedWhen: the work already has all required context and no session risk
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions agent context, startup rules, provider memory, or context degradation

registry.name: CVF Engineering Debugging And Error Recovery

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Systematic root-cause diagnosis and recovery plan.
notRecommendedWhen: there is no observed failure or diagnostic
recommendedWhen: the spec includes a failing test, broken build, exception, or unexpected behavior

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Migration plan with compatibility and rollback boundaries.
notRecommendedWhen: the change is not replacing or retiring an existing surface
recommendedWhen: the spec names deprecation, migration, compatibility, or removing old APIs

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Durable decision records and documentation for future agents.
notRecommendedWhen: the task has no decision or documentation surface
recommendedWhen: the spec asks to document architecture decisions, standards, or long-lived rationale

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Adversarial review of assumptions and high-stakes decisions.
notRecommendedWhen: the decision is low-risk and already source-verified
recommendedWhen: the spec involves high-risk decisions, uncertain assumptions, or possible overclaiming

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Production-quality UI implementation aligned with the project design contract.
notRecommendedWhen: the task is backend-only or documentation-only
recommendedWhen: the spec asks for UI, frontend components, layout, interaction, or visual polish

registry.name: CVF Governance Git Workflow And Versioning

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Clean commit and versioning flow aligned with CVF choreography.
notRecommendedWhen: no git, commit, branch, or versioning action is involved
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions commits, branches, conflicts, versioning, or material/session-sync split

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Early idea refinement into options, constraints, and a governed next move.
notRecommendedWhen: the operator already gave an exact implementation request
recommendedWhen: the spec is an early concept, brainstorm, or unclear idea that needs shaping before execution

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Small, reviewable implementation slices with validation after each step.
notRecommendedWhen: the task is a tiny single-file edit
recommendedWhen: the spec is large, multi-file, risky, or needs phased implementation

agentUseCases: ask one question at a time
selection.expectedOutputContribution: Operator clarification before risky implementation assumptions.
notRecommendedWhen: the operator request is already precise enough to execute safely
recommendedWhen: the spec is underspecified and a reasonable assumption would materially change the work

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Observability evidence for diagnosing runtime behavior.
notRecommendedWhen: the task has no runtime or diagnostic surface
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions logs, metrics, traces, alerts, monitoring, or production diagnostics

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Measurement-first performance improvement and regression control.
notRecommendedWhen: there is no performance complaint or metric
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions slow behavior, profiling, latency, Core Web Vitals, or bottlenecks

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Threat-model-first security hardening with explicit sensitive-data boundaries.
notRecommendedWhen: the task has no auth, secrets, external input, or sensitive data concern
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions auth, authorization, secrets, untrusted input, sensitive data, OWASP, or access control

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Launch readiness checklist with rollout and rollback evidence.
notRecommendedWhen: the task is not near release or rollout
recommendedWhen: the spec mentions shipping, launch, release readiness, staged rollout, monitoring, or rollback

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Source-backed decisions for framework, library, or API work.
notRecommendedWhen: the needed source facts are already governed locally
recommendedWhen: the spec depends on current framework docs, library behavior, or official source correctness

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Regression coverage and behavior proof before closure.
notRecommendedWhen: no executable test surface exists and no test harness is authorized
recommendedWhen: the spec asks to fix behavior, reproduce a bug, or prove a change with tests

registry.name: CVF Governance Skill Discovery And Invocation

selection.expectedOutputContribution: Metadata-first skill discovery and invocation boundary control.
notRecommendedWhen: the correct skill is already selected and receipt-backed
recommendedWhen: the spec asks which skill applies, how to choose skills, or how to avoid authority expansion during skill use

publicProdPackages.authority: public/prod package claim; none granted by this inventory
